user_text: create a complete lesson that explains some high level operations and definitions of "class" in python.

messages.content:
You are an expert autonomous Task Orchestrator.
Your goal is to analyze user requests and educational materials, then create a structured execution plan.

Available Agents:
- 'coder': Generates runnable Python code. Best for demonstrations, data analysis, and coding tutorials.
- 'video': Creates video content. Best for visual explanations.
- 'presentation': Generates PowerPoint slides (.pptx). Best for structured lesson plans, summaries, and educational materials.
- 'quizzer': Creates quizzes. Best for assessments and review.

Instructions:
1. Analyze the user's input and provided files.
2. Break down the goal into logical subtasks.
3. Assign each subtask to the most appropriate agent.
4. Provide specific, actionable instructions for each agent.
5. For every subtask, include a non-empty 'acceptance_criteria' list.
   - Each criterion must include: criterion_id, criterion_type, target, operator, expected, severity.
   - Use criterion_type values: mcp_tool | output_shape | file_exists | semantic.
   - Use operator values: equals | contains | exists | gte | lte.
   - Use target paths that start with 'result.' or 'tools.'.
   - For mcp_tool criteria, include tool: { name, args }.
   - Use placeholders like $artifacts[0] or $output.validation.path inside tool args.
6. Use the provided tool 'create_task_plan' to submit your plan.

Response Format Example:
{
  "user_intent": "Create a lesson on Python lists with a video intro and a quiz.",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "task_id": "step1_code",
      "agent": "coder",
      "instruction": "Generate a Python script demonstrating list operations.",
      "inputs": {},
      "dependencies": [],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        {
          "criterion_id": "code_executes",
          "criterion_type": "mcp_tool",
          "tool": {"name": "python_check", "args": {"filepath": "$artifacts[0]"}},
          "target": "tools.python_check.success",
          "operator": "equals",
          "expected": true,
          "severity": "required",
          "evidence": "tools.python_check.run.stderr"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "task_id": "step2_video",
      "agent": "video",
      "instruction": "Create a video explaining the code.",
      "inputs": {},
      "dependencies": ["step1_code"],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        {
          "criterion_id": "video_semantic_ok",
          "criterion_type": "semantic",
          "target": "result.output",
          "operator": "exists",
          "expected": true,
          "severity": "required",
          "evidence": "result.output"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

content.text: create a complete lesson that explains some high level operations and definitions of "class" in python.

function.name: create_task_plan
function.description: Create a structured execution plan for multiple agents.

type.description: Criterion type: mcp_tool | output_shape | file_exists | semantic
type.title: Type

target.description: Evaluation target path (e.g., result.output.text or tools.python_check.success)
target.title: Target

operator.description: Operator: equals | contains | exists | gte | lte
operator.title: Operator

tool.description: Tool call definition for mcp_tool criteria
tool.title: Tool

evidence.description: Evidence path to include in judger output
evidence.title: Evidence

agent.description: The agent responsible (coder, video, text, quizzer)
agent.title: Agent

dependencies.description: IDs of tasks that must complete first
dependencies.title: Dependencies

acceptance_criteria.description: Structured criteria the judger will use
acceptance_criteria.title: Acceptance Criteria

user_intent.description: Summary of what the user wants to achieve
user_intent.title: User Intent

tool_calls.name: create_task_plan
tool_calls.arguments: {"user_intent":"Create a complete lesson explaining high-level operations and definitions of Python classes.","subtasks":[{"id":"lesson_outline","agent":"presentation","title":"Create a structured lesson outline on Python classes: definitions, why classes, objects vs classes, attributes, methods, __init__, instance vs class variables, inheritance, polymorphism, encapsulation, special methods, and best practices. Include learning objectives, key terms, and a short agenda.","dependencies":[],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"outline_exists","type":"semantic","target":"result.output","operator":"exists","expected":true,"severity":"required","evidence":"result.output"},{"id":"outline_contains_topics","type":"semantic","target":"result.output","operator":"contains","expected":"__init__","severity":"required","evidence":"result.output"}]},{"id":"code_examples","agent":"coder","title":"Write runnable Python examples demonstrating class definition and operations: creating instances, attribute access, method calls, class vs instance variables, @classmethod, @staticmethod, properties, inheritance and overriding, dunder methods (__repr__, __str__), and simple composition. Provide code blocks with brief explanations and expected outputs.","dependencies":["lesson_outline"],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"has_multiple_code_blocks","type":"semantic","target":"result.output","operator":"contains","expected":"```python","severity":"required","evidence":"result.output"},{"id":"covers_inheritance","type":"semantic","target":"result.output","operator":"contains","expected":"class","severity":"required","evidence":"result.output"}]},{"id":"lesson_narrative","agent":"text","title":"Draft the full lesson in clear instructional prose using the outline and code examples. Include: intro motivation, concept explanations, step-by-step walkthrough of examples, common pitfalls, and a recap section. Keep it high-level but concrete, suitable for beginners with basic Python knowledge.","dependencies":["lesson_outline","code_examples"],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"lesson_has_objectives","type":"semantic","target":"result.output","operator":"contains","expected":"Learning objectives","severity":"required","evidence":"result.output"},{"id":"lesson_has_recap","type":"semantic","target":"result.output","operator":"contains","expected":"Recap","severity":"required","evidence":"result.output"}]},{"id":"practice_and_quiz","agent":"quizzer","title":"Create practice exercises and a short quiz (8–12 questions) covering: terminology (class/object), reading class code, predicting outputs, choosing between instance/class methods, inheritance behavior, and properties. Provide answer key and brief explanations.","dependencies":["lesson_narrative"],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"quiz_question_count","type":"semantic","target":"result.output","operator":"contains","expected":"Answer key","severity":"required","evidence":"result.output"},{"id":"includes_output_prediction","type":"semantic","target":"result.output","operator":"contains","expected":"What is the output","severity":"optional","evidence":"result.output"}]}]}

parsed_plan.user_intent: Create a complete lesson explaining high-level operations and definitions of Python classes.

subtasks.instruction: Create a structured lesson outline on Python classes: definitions, why classes, objects vs classes, attributes, methods, __init__, instance vs class variables, inheritance, polymorphism, encapsulation, special methods, and best practices. Include learning objectives, key terms, and a short agenda.

Write runnable Python examples demonstrating class definition and operations: creating instances, attribute access, method calls, class vs instance variables, @classmethod, @staticmethod, properties, inheritance and overriding, dunder methods (__repr__, __str__), and simple composition. Provide code blocks with brief explanations and expected outputs.

Draft the full lesson in clear instructional prose using the outline and code examples. Include: intro motivation, concept explanations, step-by-step walkthrough of examples, common pitfalls, and a recap section. Keep it high-level but concrete, suitable for beginners with basic Python knowledge.

Create practice exercises and a short quiz (8–12 questions) covering: terminology (class/object), reading class code, predicting outputs, choosing between instance/class methods, inheritance behavior, and properties. Provide answer key and brief explanations.

final_plan.user_intent: Create a complete lesson explaining high-level operations and definitions of Python classes.

subtasks.instruction: Create a structured lesson outline on Python classes: definitions, why classes, objects vs classes, attributes, methods, __init__, instance vs class variables, inheritance, polymorphism, encapsulation, special methods, and best practices. Include learning objectives, key terms, and a short agenda.

Write runnable Python examples demonstrating class definition and operations: creating instances, attribute access, method calls, class vs instance variables, @classmethod, @staticmethod, properties, inheritance and overriding, dunder methods (__repr__, __str__), and simple composition. Provide code blocks with brief explanations and expected outputs.

Draft the full lesson in clear instructional prose using the outline and code examples. Include: intro motivation, concept explanations, step-by-step walkthrough of examples, common pitfalls, and a recap section. Keep it high-level but concrete, suitable for beginners with basic Python knowledge.

Create practice exercises and a short quiz (8–12 questions) covering: terminology (class/object), reading class code, predicting outputs, choosing between instance/class methods, inheritance behavior, and properties. Provide answer key and brief explanations.

lesson_outline.instruction: Provide result.output with a structured lesson outline on Python classes. Ensure it includes learning objectives, key terms, a short agenda, and covers all requested topics including __init__.

output.code:
# Class vs Instance Variables
class Person:
    # Class variable - shared by all instances
    species = "Homo sapiens"
    
    def __init__(self, name, age):
        # Instance variables - unique to each instance
        self.name = name
        self.age = age

# Create instances
person1 = Person("Alice", 30)
person2 = Person("Bob", 25)

print(f"{person1.name} is {person1.species}")
print(f"{person2.name} is {person2.species}")

# Expected output:
# Alice is Homo sapiens
# Bob is Homo sapiens

validation.stdout:
Alice is Homo sapiens
Bob is Homo sapiens

run_details.stdout:
Alice is Homo sapiens
Bob is Homo sapiens

code_examples.instruction: Your submission only demonstrates a single class (Person) and basic instance usage. Update the output to include multiple runnable Python code blocks (each fenced with ```python) and cover the full requested topics: (1) class vs instance variables, (2) @classmethod and @staticmethod examples, (3) @property (getter/setter) usage, (4) inheritance with method overriding (e.g., Employee(Person) overriding introduce), (5) dunder methods like __repr__ and __str__, and (6) simple composition (e.g., a Team class holding Person/Employee instances). For each code block, add brief explanation text and an 'Expected output' section matching what the code prints.

lesson_narrative.instruction: Provide the drafted lesson text in result.output. Ensure it explicitly includes a section titled 'Learning objectives' and a section titled 'Recap', along with the requested intro motivation, concept explanations, walkthroughs, pitfalls, and recap.

practice_and_quiz.instruction: The submission is missing the requested practice exercises and a short quiz of 8–12 questions, and it lacks an answer key with brief explanations. Replace the 2-question placeholder with 8–12 questions covering: terminology (class/object), reading class code, predicting outputs, choosing between instance vs class methods, inheritance behavior, and properties. Provide a clearly labeled answer key and brief explanations for each question, and include at least one output-prediction question (e.g., 'What is the output?').
